mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate applied to equities and reduces financing costs for leveraged firms, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days when the easing path is persistent rather than a one-off cut.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive more often than negative during periods when FEDFUNDS is flat or declining, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in CPIAUCSL above recent trend forces higher expected inflation and real-rate uncertainty, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when inflation is accelerating faster than policy can respond.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its prior 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-upshift trigger events are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the average post-trigger return is non-negative across a full rolling year.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income growth and deteriorating cyclical demand, which should depress earnings expectations and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when unemployment is trending higher for multiple months.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE rise triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates falling payroll income and weaker retail demand, which should reduce near-term revenue growth expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS decline signal does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered periods fail to deliver negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO together with stable or falling HOUST indicates stronger industrial activity without a housing-led overheating shock, supporting cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive while HOUST is not accelerating
failure_condition: Reject if the combined signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than half of triggered cases, or if periods of rising INDPRO with weak HOUST consistently coincide with flat or negative equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST reflects stronger housing-cycle demand and construction activity, which boosts cyclical growth expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when housing starts are rising from a low base.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 5% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST surge triggers do not lead to positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not above zero.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, labor income momentum is deteriorating and equity earnings expectations should fall, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices growth risk quickly.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change turns negative and UNRATE rises in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor deterioration trigger is followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal loses predictive sign in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A broad demand contraction is present when GDPC1 slows, RSAFS weakens, and PAYEMS decelerates together; this combination should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because revenue, hiring, and output expectations all fall at once.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth decelerates while RSAFS and PAYEMS both weaken versus their prior 3-month averages
failure_condition: Reject if the three-way demand slowdown trigger fails to deliver negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of occurrences, or if any single proxy explains the effect better than the joint specification.
